description_long: This object is a pair of military-style helmets, each with a broad, flat brim and a rounded, dome-shaped crown. The helmets are made of a metallic material with a matte, olive-drab finish, typical of World War I or II-era Brodie helmets. One helmet features visible rivets and vent holes on the side, while the other appears smoother and more uniform in texture. The brims are wide and flat, providing ample shade and protection, and the crowns are low and gently domed. There are no visible decorative elements or insignia, giving the helmets a utilitarian and functional appearance. The overall look is robust and designed for practical use in urban or combat environments.
description: A pair of olive-drab, metallic military helmets with wide, flat brims and rounded domes, featuring utilitarian details like rivets and vent holes.
description_view_0: Side view showing both helmets, with one displaying rivets and vent holes, and the other appearing smooth and uniform.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the domed crowns and the wide, flat brims of both helmets, with rivets visible on the upper helmet.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the smooth, rounded backs of both helmets and the consistent width of the brims.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, emphasizing the vent holes and rivets on one helmet, while the other remains featureless and smooth.
